sky.role: cold indigo pre-sunrise sky, the Interior Alaska dawn before the sun clears the ridge

ground.role: frost-blue snow shadow base, with a warm peach-cream highlight (#F2DCC4) where dawn light grazes it

hero.role: ember-orange furnace-window glow: Sourdough's chest, the warmth of a local institution that planned ahead

accent.role: server-teal queue light: the cold, impersonal glow of the global data-center backlog cutting into the line

shadow.role: ash-red haze warning: the PM2.5 non-attainment overlay that floods in at the turn, the one color that must never feel decorative, it is the counterpoint made visible

palette_why: Diverges from the last 2 dispatches: 07-17 was amethyst-plum/copper/citrine/phosphor-lime (a subterranean jewel-tone drill scene); 07-18 was dawn-birch/viridian/husky-amber/graphite/crimson (a naturalistic dawn monolith). This palette shares no meaningful tokens with either. The story motivates every color: warm ember = Sourdough's earned, local competence; cold server-teal = the impersonal global AI-data-center scale that dwarfs him; ash-red = the honest, undodgeable cost (PM2.5 haze) that the competence beat must not paper over. Blue appears here ONLY as the antagonist-adjacent queue light, motivated by the literal server/data-center glow, not as a lazy default backdrop.

light.time_of_day: pre-sunrise, Interior Alaska winter dawn: sun below the horizon, sky lit but no direct sun yet
light.direction: the KEY is soft, shadowless cold-sky ambient from directly overhead (true pre-dawn, sun still below the horizon); a thin low ember-orange RIM licks in from due east (off-frame right) along the horizon line where the coming sunrise is about to break, casting no shadows of its own, only a silhouette edge
light.key_fill_rim: key = cold indigo ambient sky-fill (soft, shadowless, the pre-dawn look, no directional shadows); fill = Sourdough's own furnace-window chest acts as a PRACTICAL warm fill light on his own face and nearby snow, motivated and diegetic; rim = the low ember-orange horizon-glow rim on Sourdough's silhouette, the only directional light in the shot, reserved for edges, never used as a shadow-casting key
light.depth_approach: layered parallax: a near-frost-particle plane, a mid hero plane (Sourdough/Cell/queue), a far horizon plane (the receding order queue and distant data-center glow); ContactShadow under Sourdough's feet and RimLight per lib/lighting.tsx keep him dimensional against the flat sky
light.the_turn_light_change: at beat 10 (the PM2.5 tilt-up), the practical warm fill from Sourdough's chest visibly DIMS one stop and the ambient sky-fill desaturates toward grey-red as the HazeOverlay (new craft-advance layer, see below) settles over the scene — light itself performs the turn, not just an overlay graphic

shape_language.grammar: deliberate contrast: Sourdough, Cell, and the North Pole plant are ROUNDED, blocky, homely industrial shapes (soft rivet domes, a friendly square furnace-window face, mitten-rounded hands) reading as an approachable REGIONAL institution. The global order queue and its data-center cameo silhouettes are COLD RECTILINEAR shapes: sharp uniform crate edges, a hard grid of identical glowing racks, no faces. The haze grid at the turn is an even harder geometric overlay (a literal grid lattice) to make the federal/regulatory nature of the PM2.5 non-attainment zone legible as a shape, not just a color wash.
shape_language.why: the shape grammar carries the thesis: round+warm+local = the plant reading as a person you root for; angular+cold+global = the impersonal market squeezing him; a hard grid = the equally impersonal, equally real regulatory fact he cannot outrun.

from_manifest_for_continuity: a background Moose (fauna.tsx) for the recurring comic gag
AlaskaMini map kit for the North Pole pin and supply-line SwingSigns
HUD kit (StatBurst/BoxLabel/FatArrow/Stamp) for GE Vernova/Siemens Energy stats and the PM2.5 stamp
Character rig base for Patrice Lee (parka, worried-then-resolute emotion, arms-crossed pose)
net_new_this_run: Sourdough: the hero, a personified North Pole Power Plant characterized object (see growth mandate below) — chosen because the story needed a warm, competent, non-antagonist institutional face, and the manifest's only prior institutional character (MachineShadow) is deliberately faceless/cold, wrong register for a competence beat
Cell: a battery-storage sidekick characterized object — chosen to give the story's real parallel bet (the USDA-backed battery storage) a visual presence without a data dump
casting.diverges_from_recent_cast: does not reuse ServerMachine (the data-center antagonist from a recent dispatch) as the hero, and does not reuse MachineShadow (07-18's faceless monolith) at all in a lead role; ServerMachine appears only as a brief line-cutting cameo customer in the queue, never centered.

motion_language.how_the_world_moves: the queue is a slow lateral crawl (parallax layers drifting opposite the push); ticket-dispenser and StatBurst pops use spring anticipation+overshoot; the Moose-bump gag uses a sharp squash-stretch impact with speed lines; the two SwingSigns swing shut with a hard snap and a settle-back wobble; the haze grid ROLLS IN as a soft alpha-build over ~1.5s, not a hard cut
motion_language.dominant_camera_language_note: motion_vector is ONE axis (config/composition_axes.yaml: 'the dominant direction/kind of movement on screen'), used both for the whole-piece fingerprint and for each shot. storyboard.fingerprint.motion_vector ('push-in-only') records the piece's DOMINANT tendency, carried by its two bookend/held shots: shot 1 (Sourdough pinning the ticket) and shot 6 (Sourdough frozen mid-vote), both true slow camera pushes on a held portrait, per config's own definition of push-in-only ('near-static scene, slow camera dolly'). Shots 2-5 are DELIBERATELY DIFFERENT on this axis, and must be: shot_structure.yaml's anti-zoom rule requires >=2 of the 4 heavy axes (including motion_vector) to change between adjacent shots, or a cut reads as a zoom rather than a real scene change. So shot 2's horizontal-traverse (the pull-back reveal), shot 3's radial-emanate (stat badges popping from center), shot 4's vertical-descent (the SwingSigns slamming shut), and shot 5's vertical-rise (the tilt-up reveal) are the plan's real, motivated middle-act variety — not a contradiction of the fingerprint, but the reason the fingerprint is a DOMINANT tag, not a claim every shot is identical. The piece opens and closes push-in (identity), and varies its motion through the middle (shot structure), landing its one true camera EXCEPTION at shot 2 (a motivated pull-back to establish scale) before the shot-5 push-through-Sourdough's-chest-and-tilt-up signature move returns the piece to push language for the close.
hero_moves_named: Sourdough pinning the ticket (beat 1 / shot 1): anticipation reach -> pin -> settle, chest-glow pulse on contact

the queue pull-back (beat 2, within shot 1's 0-9s window; the reveal completes into shot 2's map-territory framing): the single camera exception, motivates the 180-degree motion blur system on the receding crates, then the camera returns to push-in language
the PM2.5 tilt-up (beat 10 / shot 5): the SIGNATURE move, a combined push-through-and-tilt that is this run's craft_advance test case and the dominant push language's payoff
Sourdough freezing mid-vote (beat 14 / shot 6): a held breath, no motion, deliberately STILL after 60s of never-rest cadence, to make the freeze read as a real freeze
motion_language.180_blur_and_juice: 180-degree motion blur on the queue pull-back and the whip-pan into the 2024/2025 door-slams; ImpactStar + a 2-4px screen-shake on both SwingSign slams and the Moose-bump; ChipShadow under every HUD stat badge so they sit IN the lit scene

composition.focal_hierarchy: Sourdough (or the current shot's hero) always occupies the lower-third centered position per the fingerprint's centered-hero-lowerthird layout; HUD stats and labels are chrome around him, never competing for center; the 9:16 AND 4:5 safe box both keep Sourdough's face and the active StatBurst inside the centered vertical strip so the 4:5 crop never amputates the story
composition.negative_space_beats: beat 2 (the queue pull-back) and beat 10 / shot 5 (the haze tilt-up) are the two beats that intentionally empty the lower frame to let scale and dread breathe
composition.signature_shot: beat 10: the camera pushes THROUGH Sourdough's glowing chest-window and tilts up into a wide shot of North Pole as a translucent red PM2.5 grid rolls over the rooftops, his warm ember light visibly dimming a stop as it happens. This is the shot the run will be remembered for and the next exemplar candidate.

craft_advance.system: lib/lighting.tsx: a new reusable HazeOverlay/atmospheric-particulate grade layer
craft_advance.what_it_does: a translucent, animated color-grade wash (grid-textured, alpha-builds over ~1.5s, desaturates the scene's warm key by one stop while it settles) that any future story needing an air-quality, smoke, or pollution beat can trigger per-scene, tied to a narrative moment rather than applied as a blanket filter
craft_advance.why_this_run: the PM2.5 non-attainment turn needed to be felt as a real environmental and lighting change, not just a red sticker slapped over the frame; building it as a reusable lighting-engine system (not a one-off overlay PNG) means the next wildfire-smoke or air-quality Alaska story inherits it for free, advancing lib/lighting.tsx per the growth mandate's 'push at least one engine system forward' requirement
craft_advance.secondary_asset_growth: net-new characterized objects Sourdough and Cell registered to the manifest; a new 'bumped-indignant-recover' comic reaction pose added to Moose in fauna.tsx (the existing-asset new-pose requirement).
